data.title: Adobe in focus as UBS notes creative AI market is 'fiercely competitive'
data.description:
Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) was in focus on Friday after investment firm UBS reiterated that the creative artificial intelligence market has become "fiercely competitive," as evidenced by two recent commercials.

Coca-Cola's (KO) holiday commercial and a recent commercial from Toys “R” Us were both produced with AI, though neither company used Adobe's AI tools to create the content, UBS analyst Karl Keirstead wrote in a note to clients. Instead, they used tools from OpenAI — which is backed by Microsoft (MSFT) — Runway and Leonardo, which is owned by Canva. (Adobe previously tried to acquire Canva competitor Figma and failed.)

Keirstead, who lowered his price target on Adobe to $475 from $525, added that Adobe is not yet at "feature parity" with the aforementioned competitors. Additionally, the AI narrative for Adobe does not look good in the interim, Keirstead said, as there is risk for the company's annual recurring revenue guidance of 11%.

And while Coca-Cola and Toys “R” Us used Adobe's tool for final editing and post-production work, there was no incremental spend. Keirstead has a Neutral rating on Adobe.

The larger issue at hand is that the Coca-Cola commercial could be the proverbial tipping point for more AI-generated content, Keirstead posited, as he pointed to the fact the Coke ad cost one-tenth of a traditional production and took just four weeks to create, with only six people involved.

"Larger and more conservative ad agencies/brands will move more slowly, smaller agencies further out on the risk curve will move faster, but it’s happening, now," Keirstead wrote.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow rise after a choppy start to 2025
data.description:
The major market averages were higher on Friday, a day after Wall Street kicked off 2025 with a choppy first trading session.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.4%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.4% and the Dow (DJI) was +0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.54%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.24%.

U.S. stocks on Thursday experienced a choppy first trading session of 2025, in which they initially jumped, then seesawed and finally ended in negative territory. Wall Street is now on a five-day losing streak.

"The first trading day has been a very poor guide to the rest of the year in recent times, so we shouldn’t extrapolate things too far," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

For U.S. equities there was a much more negative story, with the S&P 500 posting a fifth consecutive loss, driven by even deeper losses for the Magnificent 7, Allen added.

On the economic side, the December ISM Manufacturing Index is forecasted to land shortly. The economists on an average expect it to come in at 48.2.

"A U.S. business sentiment survey is due. Partisan bias gives upside risks," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

There are a couple of Federal Reserve speakers also lined up for today, including Richmond Fed President, Thomas Barkin and San Francisco Fed President, Mary Daly.

data.title: Trillion Energy CEO retires, Sean Stofer appointed as interim CEO
data.description:
• Trillion Energy International (OTCQB:TRLEF) said on Friday that the board has accepted Arthur Halleran’s resignation and retirement as Chief Executive Officer, effective December 27, 2024.
  • The company is conducting an executive search, to appoint a seasoned executive with capital markets and technical experience to lead the company as CEO.
  • Trillion said Sean Stofer P.E., is appointed as Chairman of the board, and will also assume the role of interim CEO, while the Company completes its executive search for a permanent CEO.
  • The company additionally plans to create an advisory board consisting of industry veterans and seeks to add two more directors as part of its overall transitional plan in Q1 2025.

data.title: RTX's Raytheon awarded $946 million contract
data.description:
• Raytheon, (NYSE: RTX) was awarded a $946M contract to supply Romania with additional Patriot air and missile defense systems.
  • The contract includes fire units consisting of radars, control stations and missiles.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: HNO International reduces outstanding shares of the company
data.description:
• HNO International (OTCPK:HNOI) said it has significantly reduced the outstanding shares of the company.
  • The new number of shares are roughly by 74 million shares.

data.title: Nixxy appoints Debra Chen Volpone as CEO
data.description:
• Nixxy (NASDAQ:NIXX) announced on Friday, the appointment of Debra Chen Volpone as Chief Executive Officer and a member of the Board, effective immediately.
  • Most recently, Volpone served since 2013 as Director for a leading, Los Angeles-based boutique corporate communications firm. Previously, she was president of Oxford Metrica, a UK-based financial consulting firm specializing in board advisory, due diligence for M&A, and risk analytics for banks, insurers, and asset managers.
  • Nixxy (NIXX) shares were up 0.19% in pre-market trading on Friday.

data.title: FlexShares announces liquidation of FlexShares International Quality Dividend Defensive Index Fund
data.description:
• Northern Trust’s FlexShares Exchange Traded Funds (NASDAQ:NTRS) announced the liquidation and termination of the FlexShares International Quality Dividend Defensive Index Fund (IQDE).
  • Prior to and through the close of regular trading on the NYSE Arca on or about January 21, 2025, the Fund will undertake the process of closing down and liquidating its portfolio.
  • The Fund will discontinue accepting orders for the purchase of creation units after 3:00 p.m., Eastern time, on January 17, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Energroup Holdings to acquire Cocannco
data.description:
• Energroup Holdings (OTC:ENHD) on Friday announced plans to acquire Cocannco.
  • This transaction would transform Energroup into a revenue-generating company and remove its current "shell" status, it said.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Jeffs’ Brands signs LOI to merge Fort Products with Impact Acquisitions valued at up to $12M
data.description:
• Jeffs' Brands (NASDAQ:JFBR) said on Friday it has signed a non-binding Letter of Intent for the potential merge of Fort Products, a UK-based private company, with Impact Acquisitions Corp.
  • Under the proposed share exchange agreement, Impact Acquisitions will acquire 100% of Fort Products’ shares. Jeffs Brands will receive between 75.02% and 83.29% ownership of Impact share capital, contingent upon meeting predetermined milestones.
  • The proposed acquisition is based on a total value of Impact of approximately 4.85M CAD (approximately 3.37M$US) and a total value ascribed to Fort Products, of approximately 17.12M CAD (approximately 11.91M$US).
  • Impact's board of directors will order a valuation of Fort Products, and a condition for the proposed transaction is that the value of Fort Products will not be less than a minimum amount of 14M CAD (approximately 9.73M$US).
  • Jeffs' Brands (JFBR) shares were up more than 18% in pre-market trading on Friday.

data.title: Rivian Automotive gains after topping Q4 deliveries expectations
data.description:
Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN) announced on Friday that it produced 12,727 vehicles at its manufacturing facility in Normal, Illinois in Q4 and delivered 14,183 vehicles during the same period. Both those marks were ahead of the consensus estimate of analysts.

On a full-year 2024 basis, the company produced 49,476 vehicles and delivered 51,579, which was roughly in line with management’s full-year 2024 guidance of 47,000 to 49,000 vehicles produced and 50,500 to 52,000 vehicles delivered.

The electric vehicle maker highlighted that the shortage of a shared component on the R1 and RCV platforms is no longer a constraint on its production.

Rivian Automotive (RIVN) plans to release its Q4 earnings report on February 20.

Shares of Rivian (RIVN) pushed 3.2% higher in premarket action on Friday.
